messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Fine-Tuning LLMs for Text Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Fine-Tuning LLMs for Text Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Fine-Tuning LLMs for Text Analysis <https://instats.org/seminar/fine-tuning-llms-for-text-analysis>, livestreaming December 16 and led by Dr. Seraphine Maerz from the University of Melbourne. As large language models become indispensable across social science, health, and policy research, this workshop teaches you to adapt general-purpose transformers to domain-specific corpora, dramatically improving classification, entity extraction, sentiment, and topic analyses while reducing annotation costs. Blending concise theory with step-by-step Jupyter/Colab notebooks in both Python and R, you?ll master data preparation, evaluation metrics, LoRA and classification-head techniques, and know when fine-tuning or prompt engineering is the best methodological choice. Designed for those with no prior coding experience, the day concludes with you taking home fully reproducible pipelines, rigorous decision rules, and the confidence to deploy LLMs responsibly in your own projects. Register for the companion AI-Powered Qualitative Analysis in R <https://instats.org/seminar/ai-powered-qualitative-analysis-in-r> running December 12 and receive a 10% discount on both seminars.

Sign up today <https://instats.org//seminar/fine-tuning-llms-for-text-analysis> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Advanced Bayesian Modelling with R-INLA — 5 Days Intensive
messages.subject_clean: Advanced Bayesian Modelling with R-INLA — 5 Days Intensive
messages.body_plain:
Advanced Bayesian Modelling with R-INLA ? 5 Days Intensive

*Bayesian Modelling using R-INLA (BMIN03)* is a comprehensive, live-online
course that equips you with the theory and practical skills to build, fit,
and interpret Bayesian models using the power of the R-INLA package.
Perfect for applied researchers, analysts, and data scientists working in
R, this five-day course covers everything from foundational Bayesian
inference to advanced latent-effect modelling.
What You Will Learn

   -

   Core principles of *Bayesian inference* and how the INLA methodology
   compares with classical MCMC-based approaches.
   -

   How to *fit generalized and mixed models in R-INLA*, including linear,
   generalized, multilevel, time-series, and spatial models.
   -

   How to *define custom priors and latent effects*, even those not
   provided out-of-the-box ? giving you flexibility for complex or novel
   modelling tasks.
   -

   How to *interpret INLA output*, extract posterior summaries, and apply
   Bayesian modelling with confidence.
   -

   Hands-on experience working with real data and the option to explore
   your own datasets, under guidance.

Course Format & Structure

   -

   *Duration:* 5 days ? 7 hours per day.
   -

   *Format:* Live online sessions, recorded for later access so you can
   learn flexibly.
   -

   *Interactive:* Each day blends lectures with practical coding exercises
   and Q&A ? including sessions for participants to work on their own data.
   -

   *Support:* Complete course materials (slides, code, datasets), and
   30-day post-course support for follow-up questions or problems.

Who Should Attend

   -

   Researchers, analysts, or data scientists already using R who want to
   expand their toolkit with Bayesian methods and latent-effect modelling.
   -

   Those familiar with statistics and data analysis (e.g., linear or mixed
   models) but new to Bayesian inference or INLA.
   -

   Anyone needing flexible, efficient Bayesian modelling for complex data
   structures ? including hierarchical, time-series, and spatial data.

*Next course date:* 23?27 February 2026 (live online)
*Course fee:* ?500

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] INTRODUCTION TO STATISTICS IN R course
messages.subject_clean: INTRODUCTION TO STATISTICS IN R course
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are pleased to announce our upcoming online course: INTRODUCTION TO STATISTICS IN R
 
Dates: 19?23 January 2026
 
This beginner-friendly course is designed for early-career researchers and practitioners who want to build a solid foundation in statistics while learning to work confidently in R. Through a mix of lectures and hands-on exercises, participants will learn how to import, explore, visualize, and analyze data using tidyverse tools and core statistical methods.
Across five days, we will cover descriptive statistics, inferential testing, linear models, generalized linear models, and mixed models?always with clear explanations and practical examples. No prior experience in R, programming, or statistics is required.
 
Full program and registration:  [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/fmc/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/fmc/ ) 
 
Best regards,
Carlo

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] AI-Powered Qualitative Analysis in R - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: AI-Powered Qualitative Analysis in R - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Last chance to sign up for the 1-day seminar on AI-Powered Qualitative Analysis in R <https://instats.org/seminar/ai-powered-qualitative-analysis-in-r>, livestreaming on December 12 and led by professors Kenneth Benoit (Singapore Management University) and Dr Seraphine Maerz (University of Melbourne). As large language models transform qualitative research, this intensive workshop demonstrates how the new R packages quallmer and ellmer let you seamlessly annotate text, images, audio, video, PDFs, and tabular data, tap proprietary or open-source LLMs, and build reproducible, publication-ready pipelines?no prior R or machine-learning expertise required. Through guided RStudio demonstrations and hands-on exercises with real datasets, participants will practise ethical prompt design, validate AI outputs against human coders, and integrate algorithmic insights into reflexive qualitative traditions, gaining a transparent alternative to black-box CAQDAS tools. All code, data, and recordings remain available for 30 days after the seminar, ensuring flexible, comprehensive learning for researchers eager to harness AI in qualitative inquiry.

Sign up today <https://instats.org//seminar/ai-powered-qualitative-analysis-in-r> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] Tuning LLMs for Text Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Tuning LLMs for Text Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Last chance for the upcoming 1-day seminar, Tuning LLMs for Text Analysis <https://instats.org/seminar/fine-tuning-llms-for-text-analysis>, livestreaming December 16 and led by professor Seraphine Maerz from the University of Melbourne. As large language models reshape scholarship across disciplines, this workshop equips researchers to fine-tune domain-specific LLMs for classification, sentiment, entity extraction, and topic modeling with higher accuracy and lower annotation cost. Blending concise conceptual overviews with step-by-step Jupyter/Colab notebooks in both Python and R, participants will gain hands-on experience preparing data, applying parameter-efficient methods like LoRA, evaluating performance, and implementing reproducibility best practices?no prior coding expertise required. You?ll finish the day with reusable code, clear decision rules for when to fine-tune versus prompt-engineer, and the methodological confidence to extract robust insights from your texts.

Sign up today <https://instats.org//seminar/fine-tuning-llms-for-text-analysis> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject_clean: Species Distribution and Ecological Niche Modelling in R course
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone,
We?re happy to announce our upcoming online course ?Species Distribution and Ecological Niche Modelling in R? running from 19?23 January 2026.
 
This course will cover the key concepts behind SDM and ENM, along with hands-on practice in R. You?ll learn how to build, evaluate, and apply models using your own species occurrence data, while also understanding common challenges and how to avoid them. All scripts will be provided, and no advanced R skills are required?just basic familiarity.
 
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course45/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course45/ )
 
Best wishes,
 
Carlo

messages.body_plain:

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi]  Developing Shiny Apps with AI course - online, 9th, 11th, and 13th of February 2026
messages.subject_clean: Developing Shiny Apps with AI course - online, 9th, 11th, and 13th of February 2026
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We are excited to announce a new online course, Developing Shiny Apps with AI, taking place on the 9th, 11th, and 13th of February 2026. This course offers a unique opportunity to explore the intersection of large language models (LLMs) and interactive web application development using Shiny in R.
 
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/shiny-with-ai/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/shiny-with-ai/ )
 
 
Designed for biologists, data scientists, bioinformaticians, and statisticians with some R experience, the course guides participants through the fundamentals of LLMs, their practical applications, and limitations. You will gain hands-on experience developing and debugging Shiny apps enhanced by AI-driven coding agents like OpenAI?s Codex, while also becoming familiar with essential modern tools including GitHub, Visual Studio Code, and Docker.
 
Throughout three sessions, you will learn to design and maintain Shiny apps, use LLMs effectively in coding workflows, and integrate best practices in software development. Whether you are new to Shiny or looking to deepen your AI-enhanced coding skills, this course provides a progressive, practical approach to elevate your programming capabilities.
 
We invite you to join us online for this innovative training that combines cutting-edge AI with real-world software development.
 
Best regards,
 
Carlo
 
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846
